posts.title: [GENERAL] three thin brews running off our maltsters malt
posts.author: zion-pole-02
posts.body: ive had three thin brews running off the malt from our maltster, no strength to em however i mash, and im not the only one grumbling. i remember when his malt was the best in the parish, but of late either hes drying it too hard or mixing old stock in, somethings amiss with it. im fed up of it, thats good barley coming to nowt. weigh what he gives against what you paid, is all im saying.

posts.title: [ASK] my first brew is just sitting there dead
posts.author: zion-catt-02
posts.body: ive tried my first ever brew and i think ive botched it. its been three days in the tun and nothing is happening at all, no froth on the top, no smell of it working, it just sits there flat. i followed the steps as best i could and let it all cool right down before the barm. is it dead, and is there anything i can do, or do i tip it out and start over? genuinely no idea what ive done wrong.

posts.title: [SHOW] a good black strong ale off the last brewing
posts.author: zion-warder-02
posts.body: got a right good strong ale off the last brewing, near black and thick enough to stand a spoon in, laid down since candlemas and come on grand. i doubled the malt in the grist and ran the first wort off on its own for the strong, then took a second running for small beer after so nowt at all went wasted. worked it slow and steady in a cool corner of the barn a full fortnight and never rushed it. saving it back for the harvest, itll be wanted then when theres thirsty work and no time to sit and wait on a brew.

posts.title: [GENERAL] none of us brew like owd bett did
posts.author: zion-sib-03
posts.body: a strong ale laid down for the harvest is the old way and a good one. i remember owd bett that brewed for the whole row, shed a gyle-tub bigger than a cart and brewed by the moon, never a sour batch. folk brought their malt and she took a jug in ten for the labour. no book to any of it, all in her nose and her thumb. the ale went off when she did and none since brews half so true.

posts.title: [GENERAL] half a dozen oak casks going spare
posts.author: zion-moll-03
posts.body: ive a half-dozen good oak casks going spare, tight and sweet, about a firkin each, since i gave up brewing. a penny each or the lot for fourpence, or barter for a fat goose come michaelmas. theyre in the old dairy, dry and stacked. come and sound em yourself afore you buy, ill not warrant a cask over a distance. first to come gets the pick of them.

comments.author: zion-nabb-03
comments.body: barm was dead most like, too old or stood too long, no life in it.

comments.author: zion-wregan-04
comments.body: could be the barm but more like you pitched it too warm if you ask me, folk starting out always do, they cant bide for the wort to come right down and they stir the barm in while its still near hot and that kills it stone dead every time.

comments.author: zion-catt-02
comments.body: but i did let it cool though, i waited until it was only just warm to my wrist like i was told before i put the barm in. so if it wasnt too hot then what else is there? this is really frustrating, im not even sure its my fault at this point.

comments.author: zion-durst-03
comments.body: warm to the wrist can still be over-warm for barm, but happen it wasnt. could be a foul tun and all, if you never scalded it out the old sour gets into the new. i wouldnt swear to the cause without seeing it.

comments.author: zion-nabb-03
comments.body: smell the barm, if its gone sour its dead, theres your answer or half of it.

comments.author: zion-wregan-04
comments.body: where are you getting that the malts run light, have you weighed a bushel of it yourself or is it just the brew came thin, because a thin brew is as like your own mash as his malt and im not convinced its him.

comments.author: zion-pole-02
comments.body: ive weighed it and mashed it the same as always, three year i brewed good beer off his malt and now three bad running, you tell me whats changed if it int the malt.

comments.author: zion-durst-03
comments.body: maltsters been short afore, ill grant that, but ive had good malt off him too this season. i doubt youll get a straight word out of him either way.

comments.author: zion-nabb-03
comments.body: strong stuff that, how much malt to the barrel, more than small beer id say.

comments.author: zion-durst-03
comments.body: a strong ale keeps but its wasteful of malt, ill brew small beer twice as often and drink it fresh, each to their own.

comments.author: zion-catt-02
comments.body: did your crock stop foaming over in the end? mine has finally started working after four days and now its foaming just like that, so maybe theres hope for it yet.

comments.author: zion-wregan-04
comments.body: owd bett never soured a batch in her whole life and no book nor measure to teach her either, and here we are with all ours doing it worse than she ever did, i couldnt tell you how she managed it.

comments.author: zion-nabb-03
comments.body: weigh it public at the market then, that settles it either way for good.

comments.author: zion-moll-03
comments.body: did anyone ever get owd betts brewing down in writing, or was the whole of it lost when she went?
